Option D is incorrect because the left gastric artery is a direct branch of the celiac trunk. Option E is incorrect because the right gastro-omental artery is a branch of the gastroduodenal artery.

questions.question: The portal venous system is responsible for transporting nutrient-rich blood to the liver. Which of the following anatomical regions does NOT contribute its venous drainage to the portal vein?
questions.explanation: Option E is correct because the lower half of the anal canal drains into the systemic circulation via the inferior rectal veins (tributaries of the internal iliac veins). Option A is incorrect because the cystic vein drains the gallbladder into the portal vein. Option B is incorrect because paraumbilical veins are small tributaries of the portal vein. Option C is incorrect because the upper half of the anal canal drains into the superior rectal vein, a tributary of the portal system. Option D is incorrect because the lower third of the esophagus drains into the left gastric vein, which is a portal tributary.

options.C: Upper half of the anal canal
options.E: Lower half of the anal canal

questions.question: A clinician is explaining the risk of a pleural effusion following a traumatic rupture of the spleen. Which structure lies between the diaphragmatic surface of the spleen and the left pleura?
questions.explanation: Option E is correct because the diaphragm is the physical barrier that separates the diaphragmatic surface of the spleen from the pleura and the base of the left lung. Option A is incorrect because the left kidney is related to the visceral surface of the spleen. Option B is incorrect because the stomach is related to the gastric impression on the visceral surface. Option C is incorrect because the tail of the pancreas is related to the splenic hilum. Option D is incorrect because the phrenicocolic ligament supports the spleen inferiorly but does not separate it from the pleura.

questions.question: During a splenectomy, the surgeon must carefully ligate the vessels within the lienorenal (splenorenal) ligament. Which of the following structures is found within this specific peritoneal fold?
questions.explanation: Option B is correct because the lienorenal ligament contains the splenic artery, the splenic vein, and the tail of the pancreas. Option A is incorrect because short gastric arteries are found in the gastrosplenic ligament. Option C is incorrect because the left gastro-omental artery is also found in the gastrosplenic ligament. Option D is incorrect because the left gastric vein runs along the lesser curvature of the stomach. Option E is incorrect because the superior mesenteric vein is located posterior to the neck of the pancreas.

questions.question: The thymus gland receives its primary arterial blood supply from which of the following vessels?
questions.explanation: Option C is correct because the thymus is primarily supplied by branches of the internal thoracic and inferior thyroid arteries. Option A is incorrect because the superior thyroid artery supplies the thyroid and larynx. Option B is incorrect because the pericardiacophrenic artery supplies the pericardium and diaphragm. Option D is incorrect because the brachiocephalic trunk does not typically give direct branches to the thymus. Option E is incorrect because the arch of the aorta gives off the major vessels to the head and limbs, not the thymus.

questions.question: Which of the following structures is in direct contact with the visceral surface of the spleen at its lower pole?
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the left colic flexure (splenic flexure) of the large intestine is in contact with the colic area of the spleen, which is located at the lower pole/lateral extremity. Option B is incorrect because the tail of the pancreas typically reaches the hilum of the spleen, which is more central on the visceral surface. Option C is incorrect because the left kidney relates to the intermediate area of the visceral surface, posterior to the hilum. Option D is incorrect because the fundus of the stomach relates to the large gastric area on the superior part of the visceral surface. Option E is incorrect because the diaphragm relates to the diaphragmatic surface of the spleen, not the visceral surface.

questions.question: A medical student is identifying the branches of the splenic artery. Which of the following vessels arises from the splenic artery near the hilum to supply the greater curvature of the stomach?
questions.explanation: Option B is correct because the left gastro-omental (gastroepiploic) artery is a major branch of the splenic artery that arises near the hilum and runs along the greater curvature of the stomach within the gastrosplenic ligament. Option A is incorrect because while short gastric arteries also arise from the splenic artery, they supply the fundus of the stomach. Option C is incorrect because the left gastric artery is a direct branch of the celiac trunk. Option D is incorrect because the right gastro-omental artery is a branch of the gastroduodenal artery. Option E is incorrect because the posterior gastric artery is an inconsistent branch that arises from the mid-portion of the splenic artery.

questions.question: A 25-year-old man presents with a ruptured spleen following a blunt abdominal injury. During the physical exam, he complains of sharp pain referred to the tip of his left shoulder (Kehr's sign). Which nerve is responsible for transmitting this referred pain?
questions.explanation: Option C is correct because blood from a ruptured spleen can irritate the diaphragmatic peritoneum. The sensory fibers from the central part of the diaphragm are carried by the phrenic nerve (C3, C4, C5). This pain is referred to the C3-C4 dermatomes at the shoulder. Option A is incorrect because the supraclavicular nerves provide the cutaneous innervation to the shoulder where the pain is felt, but they do not transmit the original stimulus from the diaphragm. Option B is incorrect because the vagus nerve does not provide sensory innervation to the diaphragmatic peritoneum. Option D is incorrect because the intercostal nerves supply the peripheral part of the diaphragm, and irritation there would refer pain to the thoracic wall. Option E is incorrect because the greater splanchnic nerve carries visceral pain from the upper abdominal organs, not somatic pain from the peritoneum.

questions.question: During a surgical exploration of the superior mediastinum in a child, the thymus is reflected anteriorly. Which structure is located immediately posterior to the thymus and anterior to the trachea?
questions.explanation: Option E is correct because in the superior mediastinum, the thymus lies anterior to the great vessels, including the arch of the aorta and its branches (and the left brachiocephalic vein). The arch of the aorta itself lies anterior to the trachea. Option A is incorrect because the esophagus lies posterior to the trachea. Option B is incorrect because the left recurrent laryngeal nerve loops under the arch of the aorta and ascends in the tracheoesophageal groove. Option C is incorrect because the thoracic duct lies posterior to the esophagus in the superior mediastinum. Option D is incorrect because the internal thoracic arteries lie anterior to the thymus, just lateral to the sternum.

questions.question: What is the primary hematopoietic function of the spleen during the fifth month of fetal development?
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the spleen is a major site of red blood cell production (erythropoiesis) during the second trimester of fetal life (roughly months 3 to 6). Option B is incorrect because granulopoiesis primarily occurs in the bone marrow. Option C is incorrect because thrombopoiesis (platelet production) is primarily a bone marrow function. Option D is incorrect because myelopoiesis refers to the production of all myeloid cells, which is dominated by the bone marrow later in development. Option E is incorrect because osteogenesis is the formation of bone, not a hematopoietic function.

questions.question: The splenic vein follows a relatively straight course compared to its corresponding artery. Which of the following describes its correct anatomical relationship to the pancreas?
questions.explanation: Option B is correct because the splenic vein is situated posterior to the body of the pancreas, often creating a groove in the gland. Option A is incorrect because the splenic artery, not the vein, runs along the superior border of the pancreas. Option C is incorrect because the vein is posterior, not anterior. Option D is incorrect because the vein emerges from the hilum of the spleen and stays posterior to the tail and body. Option E is incorrect because the splenic vein joins the SMV behind the neck, not within the head.

options.A: It runs along the superior border of the pancreas
options.B: It lies posterior to the body of the pancreas
options.C: It is embedded within the anterior surface of the pancreas
options.D: It passes inferior to the tail of the pancreas
options.E: It runs within the substance of the head of the pancreas

questions.question: A patient with portal hypertension develops caput medusae. This clinical sign is a result of retrograde blood flow from the portal vein into which specific tributary?
questions.explanation: Option C is correct because the paraumbilical veins run in the falciform ligament and connect the portal vein to the superficial veins of the anterior abdominal wall. In portal hypertension, these veins dilate, creating the "caput medusae" appearance. Option A is incorrect because the superior rectal vein is involved in the formation of internal hemorrhoids. Option B is incorrect because the left gastric vein is involved in the formation of esophageal varices. Option D is incorrect because the azygos vein is a systemic vein that receives blood from esophageal varices, but it is not a portal tributary. Option E is incorrect because the inferior mesenteric vein does not have a direct cutaneous anastomosis on the abdominal wall.

questions.question: The thymus gland undergoes significant changes in composition as a person ages. In an elderly individual, what is the predominant tissue type found in the site previously occupied by the thymus?
questions.explanation: Option D is correct because the thymus undergoes "age involution" starting at puberty, where the lymphoid tissue is gradually replaced by fat (adipose tissue). Option A is incorrect because cartilage is not involved in thymic involution. Option B is incorrect because while some fibrous tissue is present, the primary replacement is fatty. Option C is incorrect because muscle does not replace lymphoid tissue. Option E is incorrect because serous membranes line cavities and do not replace solid organs.

questions.question: During a splenectomy, the surgeon identifies the tail of the pancreas. In which of the following structures is the tail of the pancreas located as it approaches the splenic hilum?
questions.explanation: Option E is correct because the lienorenal (splenorenal) ligament connects the spleen to the left kidney and contains both the splenic vessels and the tail of the pancreas. Option A is incorrect because the gastrosplenic ligament contains the short gastric and left gastro-omental vessels. Option B is incorrect because the greater omentum hangs from the greater curvature of the stomach. Option C is incorrect because the lesser omentum connects the liver to the stomach and duodenum. Option D is incorrect because the falciform ligament connects the liver to the anterior abdominal wall.

questions.question: The splenic artery is the largest branch of the celiac trunk. As it travels toward the spleen, which of the following structures lies immediately posterior to the artery?
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the splenic artery arises from the celiac trunk at the level of T12/L1 and runs across the left crus of the diaphragm and the left kidney as it passes to the left. Option B is incorrect because the stomach lies anterior to the splenic artery, separated by the lesser sac. Option C is incorrect because the lesser sac lies anterior to the splenic artery (the artery forms part of the posterior wall of the lesser sac). Option D is incorrect because the transverse colon lies inferior to the pancreas and splenic artery. Option E is incorrect because the left lobe of the liver lies anterior and to the right of the splenic artery's course.
